name: K&R
description: Kernighan and Ritchie style places the opening brace on the same line as the control statement. Commonly used in C, C++, Java, Go, and JavaScript. It offers a compact visual structure and is widely adopted as a default formatting style in many language communities.

name: Allman
description: Allman style places the opening brace on a new line, vertically aligning blocks for greater visual clarity. Often seen in C#, classic C++, and some open source C projects. It improves readability in deeply nested code by making the block structure more apparent.

name: 1TBS
description: One True Brace Style (1TBS) is similar to K&R, but enforces that 'else' and 'else if' statements appear on the same line as the closing brace of the previous block. Commonly used in JavaScript and C projects. It combines consistency with compactness while ensuring clear block boundaries.
